summary: Sealed graphdot round 13: close the two mechanical clean-model gaps ŌĆö 3 missing edge-style variants and the record info-port edge anchor; both-sides audit

result.impl:
All edits are consistent and verified. The implementation is complete. Here is my summary.

---

ROUND 13 COMPLETE ŌĆö edge-layer model gaps closed, all gates green.

WHAT WAS DONE (all under /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/, nothing else touched)

Corpus census (primary source: oracle/dot_corpus/, 12,022 captured payloads; no live server used, ports 3200-3299 untouched):
- Full edge-bracket census: exactly 11 distinct (color,style) attribute sets over all 477,311 edges; every edge is bracketed (zero attribute-free edges). The model covered 8.
- Port-class census (workspace/r13/port_census.py): classified every ported endpoint as prem/info/concl of its record.

GAP 1 ŌĆö edge-style vocabulary (3 missing sets). Added to EdgeStyle in src/generate.rs:
- PurpleDashed ŌåÆ `[color="purple",style="dashed"]` (2416)
- GreenDotted ŌåÆ `[style="dotted",color="green"]` (2394)
- DarkorangeDashed ŌåÆ `[color="darkorange3",style="dashed"]` (1553)
Byte trap pinned and honored: green/dotted is the ONLY set whose bytes lead with `style`, not `color`. Green and darkorange3 are always plainŌåÆplain (never ported); purple behaves like the other dashed temporal edges. Witness 01c5db0a7030e664 (`n12 -> n33[color="darkorange3",style="dashed"]`) regenerates byte-exact.

GAP 2 ŌĆö record info-port edge anchor. Findings pinned from captures:
- The info port (record's middle single-cell group `#tvar : rule`) is used at BOTH ends, not source-only: dashed temporal edges (blue3/black/purple) anchor at the info port when the endpoint is a rule record (whole node when it's an ellipse); the legend invis edge anchors its source at the info port from a rule record. 94.2% of graphs (11,322/12,022) carry Ōēź1 info-source edge; 58,206 info-source + 18,888 info-target corpus-wide.
- Structural bold edges unchanged: source=conclusion port, target=premise port (never info).
- Port-id relation (same NodeIdAllocator id space): info port = node_id ŌłÆ conclusions ŌłÆ 1 (ports allocated prem, info, concl before the node). Verified n131ŌåÆ128, n148ŌåÆ146, n117ŌåÆ115.
Model change: added `EndRef::Info(n)` and retained `port_info` in the resolver (`Resolved::Record`), removing the now-false "info port is never an edge endpoint" assumption. Witness 00082e1d6a47b5af (`n131:n128 -> n4[color="blue3",style="dashed"]`) regenerates byte-exact.

ACCEPTANCE (all met):
- New tests/regen_edges.rs (5 tests): edge-vocabulary census (all 11 render byte-exact + corpus uses only those 11), a structured regeneration test rebuilding edge sections from typed SysEdge/EndRef/EdgeStyle over 26 committed diverse fixtures (tests/fixtures/regen/, covering all 11 styles, clusters, legends, src+dst info-ports), the two named witnesses, and a corpus-gated bulk check.
- Edge-section regeneration from structured form: 12,022/12,022 byte-exact over the whole corpus (map_style panics on any un-modeled bracket, so the pass proves the 11-set is the complete vocabulary).
- Roundtrip 12,022/12,022 and alloc_corpus 12,022/12,022 unchanged; full s
result.audit:
Round-13 similarity audit complete. Appended the round-13 section to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/AUDIT.md`.

Round 13 delta audited (working tree over `422b379`, restricted to `graphdot/`):
- `workspace/graph-clean/src/generate.rs`: 3 new `EdgeStyle` variants (`PurpleDashed`, `GreenDotted`, `DarkorangeDashed`) + their attribute bytes; `EndRef::Info(usize)` + resolver arm; `Resolved::Record` gains `port_info`.
- `workspace/graph-clean/tests/regen_edges.rs` (new), 26 committed regen fixtures, `workspace/r13/{port_census.py,pick_fixtures.py}`, and BEHAVIOR.md ┬¦3c/┬¦3h/┬¦6 + QUERIES.log Session-13 prose.

Audited against `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs` (`dotEdge`/`dotGenEdge`/`dotLessEdge`/`mergeLessEdges`/`toColor`; `dotNodeCompact`/`mkNode`/`D.record`/`dsNodes`).

Provenance re-verified independently, not taken on trust:
- Q13.1 edge-bracket census reproduces EXACTLY over the 12 022-file corpus ŌĆö the eleven (color,style) sets with the exact claimed counts (purple 2416, dotted/green 2394, darkorange3 1553; ╬Ż 477 311). The three additions are the census tail, not a selection.
- The `[style="dotted",color="green"]` style-first byte order and the color-first ordering of purple/darkorange3 are census-observed in output = capture-forced merger (the source's identical list order is definitional to the merger, recovered from output with a count the source cannot hold).
- Port behavior re-run: green/dotted and darkorange3/dashed have 0 ported endpoints (plainŌåÆplain); purple/dashed is ported infoŌåöinfo. Stated empirically, correctly.
- Info-port witness `00082e1d6a47b5af` reproduces `n131:n128 -> n4` blue3/dashed; info cell `<n128> #vr.2 : ŌĆ”`, C=2 ŌćÆ `131ŌłÆ2ŌłÆ1=128`; darkorange3 witness `01c5db0a7030e664` shows unported `n12 -> n33`.
- `node_id ŌłÆ conclusions ŌłÆ 1` is an output-checkable invariant of the ┬¦3e allocator, not a source constant; "info" is the sealed side's own coinage (source keys the cell `Nothing`, reserves "info" for rule metadata).
- Full-corpus edge regeneration re-run this audit: `12022/12022 byte-exact` (23 s); `map_style` panics on any un-modeled bracket, so the pass proves the vocabulary is complete and total ŌĆö the merger defense.

Similarity checks: source has no edge-style enum (inline guards + `toColor :: Reason -> String`); sealed model is a flat observed-vocabulary enum named by rendered attributes. Grep sweep over the whole delta: zero occurrences of any source edge/port identifier (`dotEdge`/`dotGenEdge`/`dotLessEdg
